title: Iran Begins Long Funeral Procession for Late Supreme Leader
subtitle: Massive crowds gather in Tehran as the country marks the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei four months after he was killed.

summary.en: Iran has started a seven-day funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The leader died in February during airstrikes conducted by the United States and Israel. Large crowds are gathering in Tehran to pay their respects and show support for the current government. Foreign leaders from several nations have arrived to attend the ceremonies. Meanwhile, many Iranians remain divided over the legacy of the leader who held power for decades.
summary.zh: 伊朗已為已故最高領袖阿亞圖拉阿里·哈米尼舉行七天的葬禮。這位領袖於二月在美國與以色列發動的空襲中喪生。大批民眾聚集在德黑蘭表達哀悼，並展現對現任政府的支持。多國外國領袖已抵達參加儀式。與此同時，許多伊朗人對於這位掌權數十年的領袖之歷史評價仍存有分歧。

paragraphs.en: Four months after the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran has begun a massive, seven-day funeral process in the capital city of Tehran. The leader was killed on February 28 during airstrikes launched by the United States and Israel at the start of the ongoing war. According to NPR News, the ceremonies were delayed for several months due to the difficult conditions caused by the conflict.
paragraphs.zh: 在最高領袖阿亞圖拉阿里·哈米尼（Ayatollah Ali Khamenei）逝世四個月後，伊朗已在首都德黑蘭展開為期七天的盛大葬禮。這位領袖是在二月二十八日，於美國與以色列發動空襲、戰爭剛爆發時喪生。根據美國國家公共廣播電台（NPR）報導，由於衝突造成的艱困局勢，這些儀式延宕了數月之久。

paragraphs.en: Large crowds have gathered at the Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla, a major prayer complex in Tehran, to visit the casket of the late leader. The caskets of four of his family members, who were also killed in the same strikes, were displayed alongside his. During the events, many mourners wore black and expressed their grief openly. Some people chanted slogans against the United States, while others carried flags representing groups like the Lebanese militia Hezbollah. A spokesperson for the funeral organizers stated that the event is intended to show the world that the Iranian government remains stable and maintains strong public support.
paragraphs.zh: 大批民眾聚集在德黑蘭的主要祈禱場所「伊瑪目霍梅尼大清真寺」（Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla），瞻仰這位已故領袖的靈柩。與他一同在空襲中喪生的四名家屬，其靈柩也並列展示。活動期間，許多哀悼者身著黑衣，公開表達悲痛。部分民眾高喊反美口號，其他人則揮舞著代表黎巴嫩民兵組織「真主黨」（Hezbollah）等團體的旗幟。葬禮主辦單位發言人表示，此活動旨在向世界展示伊朗政府依然穩定，並維持著強大的民意支持。

paragraphs.en: Foreign officials have traveled to Tehran to participate in the ceremonies. NPR News reports that representatives from several countries, including Russia, Pakistan, Iraq, and Afghanistan, attended the events as the Ayatollah lay in state. A delegation from Hamas was also present. Notably, Saudi Arabia, which has had a long history of tension with Iran, sent a group to attend the funeral as well. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and the speaker of parliament, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, were seen praying near the caskets. However, the current leader and son of the late Ayatollah, Mojataba Khamenei, has not been seen in public since he took over the position in March.
paragraphs.zh: 外國官員已前往德黑蘭參與儀式。NPR 報導指出，當阿亞圖拉的靈柩停放供人瞻仰時，包括俄羅斯、巴基斯坦、伊拉克與阿富汗在內的多國代表皆出席了活動。一個哈瑪斯（Hamas）代表團也到場致意。值得注意的是，與伊朗長期關係緊張的沙烏地阿拉伯，也派遣了一個代表團參加葬禮。伊朗總統裴澤斯基安（Masoud Pezeshkian）與國會議長卡利巴夫（Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf）被目擊在靈柩旁祈禱。然而，現任領袖、即已故阿亞圖拉之子莫吉塔巴·哈米尼（Mojataba Khamenei），自三月接任職位以來，尚未在公開場合露面。

paragraphs.en: While the government seeks to project unity, the legacy of the late Supreme Leader remains a point of deep disagreement among the Iranian people. Ayatollah Khamenei had been in power since 1989 and was responsible for the growth of the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps, known as the IRGC. According to the Iranian human rights group HRANA, the IRGC was responsible for the deaths of at least 7,000 people during nationwide protests that began in December 2025. These protests were held against the government, and the memory of the violence continues to affect many citizens today.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管政府試圖展現團結，但這位已故最高領袖的歷史評價在伊朗民眾之間仍存在嚴重分歧。阿亞圖拉·哈米尼自一九八九年掌權，並一手推動了伊朗革命衛隊（IRGC）的擴張。根據伊朗人權組織 HRANA 指出，革命衛隊在二零二五年十二月爆發的全國性抗議活動中，造成至少七千人死亡。這些抗議是針對政府而發起，而當時暴力的記憶至今仍影響著許多公民。

paragraphs.en: For some Iranians, the funeral ceremonies bring little comfort. One 32-year-old woman, who spoke to NPR News on the condition of anonymity due to fear of government punishment, said that the leader continues to cause pain even after his death. Her brother was killed during the protests earlier this year. Other citizens, such as a 33-year-old woman named Fatmeh, described how the death of the leader has caused arguments within families. Fatmeh explained that her own mother views the Ayatollah as a central part of her religious life and fully supports the funeral, even as others in the family feel differently.
paragraphs.zh: 對部分伊朗人而言，葬禮儀式帶來的慰藉微乎其微。一名因擔心政府懲罰而要求匿名的三十二歲女性向 NPR 表示，這位領袖即便在死後仍持續造成痛苦。她的兄弟在今年稍早的抗議活動中喪生。其他公民，例如一名三十三歲、名為法特梅（Fatmeh）的女性，描述了這位領袖的死如何在家庭內部引發爭執。法特梅解釋說，她自己的母親將阿亞圖拉視為宗教生活的核心，並全力支持這場葬禮，儘管家中其他人持有不同看法。

paragraphs.en: As the week continues, mourners will be able to visit the site in Tehran until the end of the weekend. On Monday, the body will be moved as part of a larger procession. The government hopes that these events will demonstrate the strength of the Islamic revolutionary power, but the mixed reactions from the public highlight a country that is still struggling with the effects of the war and its own internal divisions. The world is watching closely to see how this transition of power affects the future of the region, especially as the war between Iran and its opponents continues to influence daily life.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著本週持續進行，哀悼者將能在週末結束前前往德黑蘭的現場瞻仰。週一，遺體將移靈進行更大規模的遊行。政府希望這些活動能展現伊斯蘭革命政權的力量，但民眾的兩極反應凸顯出這個國家仍在與戰爭的後果及內部分裂搏鬥。全世界正密切關注權力交接將如何影響該地區的未來，特別是伊朗與其對手之間的戰爭持續影響著日常生活之際。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be placed in a public place so that people can pay their respects before a funeral.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （重要人物遺體）停靈供大眾瞻仰。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個固定搭配的慣用語，容易誤以為是「躺在國家裡」。文中：NPR News reports that representatives from several countries, including Russia, Pakistan, Iraq, and Afghanistan, attended the events as the Ayatollah lay in state.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To present or promote a particular image or feeling to others.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 展現、投射（某種形象或感覺）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（計畫），這裡作動詞，指刻意向外界展示某種形象。文中：While the government seeks to project unity, the legacy of the late Supreme Leader remains a point of deep disagreement among the Iranian people.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To assume control or responsibility for something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由 take 和 over 組成，意思與原意差異大，指接替職位或權力。文中：However, the current leader and son of the late Ayatollah, Mojataba Khamenei, has not been seen in public since he took over the position in March.

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: Dayslong funeral for slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei begins in Tehran

previousArticles.title: Iran Begins Large Funeral Ceremonies for Late Supreme Leader
previousArticles.summaryEn: Iran has started a multi-day funeral process for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The leader was killed in February during airstrikes by the United States and Israel. Huge crowds gathered in Tehran to mourn and show support for the government. Foreign leaders from several nations attended the early ceremonies to pay their respects. The event is being used by the Iranian government to demonstrate national unity and strength.

previousArticles.title: Iran Prepares for Days of Mourning Following Death of Supreme Leader
previousArticles.summaryEn: Iran is preparing for a massive, multi-day funeral ceremony for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Millions of people are expected to participate in events across the country to honor the leader who died following a recent war. Foreign dignitaries from several nations have arrived in Tehran to pay their respects. The government hopes this show of unity will strengthen its position in ongoing international negotiations. Meanwhile, the country faces a leadership transition as the late leader's son, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, takes over.

previousArticles.title: Iran Prepares for Massive Funeral Following Death of Supreme Leader
previousArticles.summaryEn: Iran is organizing a multi-day funeral ceremony for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Millions of people are expected to join the events in Tehran and other cities. Foreign leaders from several countries have arrived to pay their respects. The government hopes these events will show strength during a difficult time. The new leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, remains in hiding following injuries from a recent attack.

quiz.question: Which of the following describes the presence of the late Ayatollah's family members at the funeral?

options.A: They were buried in a private ceremony before the public event.
options.B: Their caskets were placed next to the Ayatollah's casket during the event.
options.C: They were seen leading the funeral procession alongside the new leader.
options.D: They were absent from the ceremony due to security concerns.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，文中明確提到 Ayatollah 的四位家人在同一場空襲中喪生，且他們的靈柩與 Ayatollah 的靈柩並排展示。選項 A 與文意不符，因為文中並未提及私下埋葬；選項 C 錯誤，因為文中提到現任領導人 Mojataba Khamenei 並未公開露面；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中明確指出家人靈柩在場。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current political climate in Iran based on the text?

options.A: The public is universally supportive of the government's transition of power.
options.B: The government has successfully silenced all opposition through the funeral events.
options.C: There is a significant divide between government-led displays of unity and the personal experiences of many citizens.
options.D: The international community has completely withdrawn its support for the Iranian government.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第四段與第五段對比了政府試圖展現的團結（project unity）與民眾內心的痛苦與分裂（internal divisions）。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到民眾反應兩極；選項 B 錯誤，因為文中提到民眾仍因抗議事件感到痛苦，並未被「噤聲」；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中提到多國代表出席了葬禮，顯示並未完全撤回支持。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does the phrase 'lay in state' mean?

options.A: To be displayed publicly in a coffin before burial.
options.B: To be hidden from the public to ensure safety.
options.C: To be officially declared dead by the government.
options.D: To be resting in a private, secure location.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。片語 'lay in state' 指的是政要或重要人物去世後，靈柩公開供大眾瞻仰的儀式。文中提到外國官員前往 Tehran 參加儀式，正是為了瞻仰 Ayatollah 的靈柩。選項 B 與文意相反；選項 C 雖然是事實，但該片語專指「瞻仰儀式」而非「宣告死亡」；選項 D 則忽略了該詞彙在外交禮儀中「公開供人弔唁」的特定含義。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: A detailed biography of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's long political career.
options.B: A report on the military strategy used by the United States and Israel during the war.
options.C: An analysis of how the Iranian government will change its foreign policy.
options.D: A description of the Ayatollah's funeral and the underlying social tensions it reveals in Iran.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。這篇文章的核心在於描述 Ayatollah 的葬禮儀式，並同時強調了伊朗社會內部的分歧與民眾對政府的不滿。選項 A 僅是背景資訊；選項 B 偏離主題，文章未深入探討軍事戰略；選項 C 雖然提及權力轉移，但並未對未來的外交政策進行分析。因此 D 是最能涵蓋全文主旨的選項。
